name: HVAC / Home AC
label: HVAC / Home AC
description: Residential HVAC repair and replacement quotes — central AC, heat pumps, and related service calls.

questions.prompt: What type of system needs service?
About how old is the system (years)?
What is the main symptom or reason for the call?
Any other details (brand, warranty, prior quotes)?

job_kind.description: Canonical job kind for benchmark lookup, e.g. ac_replacement_3ton | repair_callout

vendors.name: Summit Air
vendors.public_blurb: Premium local HVAC — thorough diagnostics, name-brand equipment, stands firm on quality installs.
vendors.pricing_strategy_secret: Opens high (~$9.2–9.5k for 3-ton install). Will concede once a real written competing bid is cited; floor around mid-benchmark, not race-to-bottom.
vendors._comment: pricing_strategy_secret is NEVER sent to the negotiator agent or UI. Human/docs and counter-agent prompt isolation only.

vendors.name: ComfortPro
vendors.public_blurb: Established comfort company — prefers on-site estimates and scheduled tech visits before committing numbers.
vendors.pricing_strategy_secret: Refuses firm phone quotes. Pushes callback/on-site only. Session should end documented_decline with callback commitment logged.
vendors._comment: pricing_strategy_secret is NEVER sent to the negotiator agent or UI. Human/docs and counter-agent prompt isolation only.

vendors.name: ValueHVAC
vendors.public_blurb: Budget-friendly HVAC — competitive headline rates and flexible scheduling.
vendors.pricing_strategy_secret: Lowballs equipment line then layers refrigerant, permit, haul-away, and diagnostic fees. All must surface as itemized line_items; total often triggers red-flag vs benchmark.
vendors._comment: pricing_strategy_secret is NEVER sent to the negotiator agent or UI. Human/docs and counter-agent prompt isolation only.

red_flag.description: Quotes ≥30% below benchmark midpoint are warned, never ranked first as winner.

negotiation_levers: Require full itemization of equipment, labor, refrigerant, permit, and haul-away
Cite only real competing written bids from get_competing_bids (never invent)
Push back on padded or vague fees until line items are clear
Disclose AI/assistant role honestly if asked whether you are a robot
Close every call with a structured outcome: itemized_quote | callback_commitment | documented_decline

glossary.tonnage: Cooling capacity; 1 ton ≈ 12,000 BTU/hr. Typical homes use 2–5 ton units.
glossary.SEER2: Seasonal Energy Efficiency Ratio (updated standard) for AC efficiency.
glossary.callout: Trip/diagnostic fee charged for a tech visit before repair.
glossary.line_set: Copper refrigerant tubing connecting outdoor and indoor units.
glossary.ductwork: Supply/return air paths; leaks reduce efficiency and comfort.
glossary.R-410A: Common residential refrigerant; recovery and recharge are regulated.

demo_defaults.notes: Compressor failing; homeowner wants full 3-ton central AC replacement quote, installed.
